mechanism_narrative: A downward turn in FEDFUNDS lowers the equity discount rate and lifts valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when the policy rate is falling. The signal fails if rate cuts occur only after market stress that overwhelms the discount-rate effect.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS declines for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the triggered hit rate falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent low marks weakening household income and higher recession risk, which compresses equity prices within one month. The mechanism is strongest when unemployment is still rising rather than merely elevated.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered sample does not produce negative 20-day forward returns more often than positive returns, or if SPX gains in more than 60% of unemployment-rise triggers.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly growth in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer spending, supporting revenue expectations and near-term equity gains. The signal is positive when retail sales accelerate relative to the prior three months and fails when sales gains are driven by nominal price increases without broad demand improvement.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and above its prior 12-month median change
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS acceleration does not correspond to higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the rank correlation stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in INDPRO and HOUST signals broad industrial and housing weakness, implying slower real activity and weaker earnings expectations that depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails if lower production is offset by easier monetary policy that dominates market pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous 3-month declines in INDPRO and HOUST do not reduce 20-day SPX returns versus non-signal periods, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak while PAYEMS continues to slow, the economy is entering a demand-led slowdown that should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter as earnings estimates are revised down. The signal fails if labor softness is quickly reversed or if GDP revisions erase the apparent slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDP-growth plus slowing payrolls do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal underperforms a naive baseline in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% suppresses equity multiples by keeping real discount rates elevated, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism fails when the market has already fully priced the restrictive stance and rates are unchanged.
failure_condition: Reject if sessions with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show lower 5-day forward SPX returns than sessions below the threshold, or if the direction flips positive after controlling for the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST identifies a consumption-plus-housing upswing that supports cyclical revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when housing strength is isolated but retail sales remain weak.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and HOUST do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined interaction is not stronger than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment environment, an uptick in PAYEMS after prior softness confirms labor-market stabilization and supports risk assets through better income expectations. The effect is positive only when UNRATE is already low; it fails in recessionary labor markets where hiring gains are too weak to change earnings outlooks.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns positive while UNRATE is below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-unemployment plus payroll-reacceleration condition does not improve 20-day SPX returns, or if positive outcomes are not more frequent than negative outcomes under the trigger.
